content_en:
When one supplicates quietly and in secret, he is more likely to feel concentrated, with his heart fully preoccupied in thoughts of supplication and humility. On the other hand, when one raises his voice, his heart is likely to become distracted.

When a person speaks quietly, he shows that he feels a sense of closeness to the one he is communicating with, as opposed to a person calling out in a loud voice to someone who is at a distance, This is why Allah 3 praised His slave Zakariyyah %8, with His saying:

“When he called out his Lord (Allah) - a call in secret’ (Qur’an 19:3)

A person who supplicates quietly and in secret is likely to supplicate for a longer period of time than a person who supplicates out loud; the latter might get bored of talking out loud, he might feel dryness in his mouth and stop, or his tongue might get tired quickly, and so he will stop.

Sometimes, it is necessary to keep certain blessings hidden, because for every blessing there is a jealous person, whose jealousy corresponds in degree to the greatness of the blessing. And one of the greatest of blessings is being guided to supplicate to Allah 38; hence it is better to invoke Allah 3% in secret.

Chapter - 22 The Manners Of Supplication 479

9) To Supplicate With An Alert And Attentive Heart Leads To A Person’s Supplications Being Answered

When one invokes Allah 3@ with a heart that is awake and alert,

he is more likely to have his supplications answered. This means

that one should read invocations with, among other things, a

sense of fear, hope, and ete Allah 3€ said:

“Invoke your Lord with sont and in secret.” And Allah %¢ said:

as OB ABS) “And invoke Him with fear and hope’’ (Quran 7:56) And the Prophet 2% said,

be tS Cots Vat OF bg BENG Off 815 a 1250 GY pe i

“When you invoke Allah, you should believe with certainty that your supplication will be answered. And know that Allah does not answer a supplication from a heart that is inattentive and heedless,""1

10) It Is Recommended To Invoke Allah 3€ Persistently And Repeatedly

Again, one should take the necessary steps to improve the likelihood of his supplications being answered. One such step is to invoke Allah % with persistence, repeatedly making the same supplication over and over again, showing one’s humility and complete dependence upon his Lord. It is said that whoever knocks a door often enough will sooner or later be let inside.

‘Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab 4 said, “When the day of Badr came to pass, the Messenger of Allah #% looked at the disbelievers, who

"I Related by At-Tirmidhee (3479), and Al-Albaanee mentioned it in AsSilsilatus-Saheehah (594).

were 1000 in number, while his companions were 319 men. The Prophet of Allah #¢ then faced the Qiblah, extended his hands, and then began to call out to his Lord: ‘O Allah, carry out for me that which You promised me. O Allah, bring to pass that which You promised me. O Allah, if You destroy this group from the people of Islam, You will not be worshipped on earth.’ He continued to call out to his Lord, facing the Qiblah, and with his hands extended, until his robe fell down from his shoulders. Then Abu Bakr # went to him, took his robe, and flung it back onto his shoulders. Abu Bakr 4 then hugged him from behind and said, ‘O Prophet of Allah, sufficient for you is your supplicating and calling out to your Lord, for He will complete for you that which He promised you. Then Allah ‘Azza Wa-Jall (Mighty and Majestic is He!) revealed: {(Remember) when you sought help of your Lord and He answered you (saying): “I will help you with a thousand of the angels each behind the other (following one another) in succession.”}. So Allah helped him with the angels..."

Abu Hurairah 4s related that At-Tufail Ibn ‘Amr Ad-Dausee went to the Messenger of Allah #% and said, “Verily, the Daus (tribe) has disobeyed and refused (to accept Islam), so invoke Allah against them.” The Messenger of Allah #% then faced the Qiblah and raised his hands; meanwhile the people were saying, “The people (referring to the Daus tribe) are surely destroyed.” But the Messenger of Allah 3 made this supplication:

tag, 1555 38 a pg lbs te38 81 Gan “O Allah, guide the [people of the] Daus [tribe] and bring them.

O Allah, guide Daus and bring them (to the Messenger of Allah 3% and to Islam).""7)

'l Related by Muslim (1763), Ahmad (208), and At-Tirmidhee (3081). 1 Related by Bukhaaree (2937), Muslim (2524), and Ahmad (7273). And the above-mentioned wording is from Ahmad’s narration of the hadith.

Chapter - 22 The Manners Of Supplication 481

11) To Ask With Determination And Resolve

When one invokes Allah 4, he should show a sense of determination and resolve, which means that he should not make the answer of his supplication hinge upon Allah’s will, by saying, ‘“O Allah, grant me such and such if you will.” Instead one should show resolve, being sure that Allah 3¢ will answer his supplication. By being determined and sure in one’s supplications, one shows that he places his trust with Allah 3%, that he is invoking the All-Seeing and the All-Hearing, that His Lord is upon all things able, and that nothing in the heavens and earth can weaken, thwart, or defeat Allah 3¢.

Anas related that the Messenger of Allah # said,

gb Sieh Be Vy ced 3 ited ai yes Bp “When you invoke Allah, then be determined in your

supplication. And do not let one of you say, ‘If You will, then give me,’ for indeed, none can coerce Allah.”

Muslim related the hadith with this wording: “But let him be determined in asking and let him ask for

something great (such as Paradise), for nothing that Allah 3% gives is much or great for Him.’

“Then be determined in your supplication” can mean, “Be resolved and do not hesitate”; some scholars say that it means, “Be resolved and do not be weak in asking”; and yet others say that it means, “Think well of Allah, being sure that He will answer your supplications.” By saying, ““O Allah, give me such and such if you will,” one seems to say that he doesn’t really need either Allah to help him or the thing he is asking for.

"] Related by Bukhaaree (7464), Muslim (2678, and the second narration:

12) It ls Recommended To Preface One’s Supplications With Praises For Allah 3 And Then The Sending Of prayers Upon His Messenger 2%

Scholars unanimously agree that it is recommended to first praise

and extol Allah 3@ and send prayers upon the Messenger of Allah

3 before and after one supplicates. Fudaalah Ibn ‘Abeed 4 said,

“The Messenger of Allah # heard a man supplicate in his prayer,

without glorifying Allah and without sending prayers upon the

Prophet #%. The Messenger of Allah #% then said [to him],

“You have rushed [your supplication], O worshipper. When you pray and then sit, praise Allah with praises He is deserving of and send prayers upon me; then invoke Him.’

Then another man prayed after that; he praised Allah and sent prayers upon the Prophet #%. The Prophet # said,

“O worshipper, supplicate, you will be answered.’”"]

{] Related by At-Tirmidhee (3476), who said, “This hadith is Hasan’; by

Chapter - 22 The Manners Of Supplication 483

Ibn Mas’ood & related a similar narration, in which he 4 said, “I was once praying while Abu Bakr and ‘Umar # were with the Prophet #. When I sat down, I began by praising Allah and then sending prayers upon the Prophet #%. I then supplicated for myself. The Prophet # then said,

“Ask, you will be given; ask, you will be given.””")

13) Asking For One's Supplications To Be Answered By Dint Of Previously Performed Good Deeds

One increases the chances of his supplication being answered when one mentions a previously performed good deed prior to supplicating. The Prophet #% related the story of three men, who were stuck in a cave when a large rock blocked off its entrance. They said to one another, “Think over the righteous deeds you performed for Allah, and then invoke Allah by them; perhaps Allah will [then] make an opening for us [to leave].”” Ahmad related the narration with this wording: “Then let each man invoke by the best deed he performed; perhaps Allah will save us from this [predicament].”"] Next, one of them mentioned the best deed he performed, after which he invoked Allah 3€; the other two followed suit, and then Allah 3 answered their supplications and saved them from destruction.

14) It Is Recommended To Invoke Allah 3 With Supplications Whose Meanings Are Comprehensive Of All That Is Good

Supplications that are most comprehensive in their meanings of goodness are those supplications that are found in the Qur’an

Abu Daawood (1481); by An-Nassaaee (1284), and the above-mentioned wording is from his narration of the hadith. In grading the hadith, A\- Albaanee said, ““sahih.’ (1217)

1 Related by At-Tirmidhee (593); the above-mentioned wording is from his narration of the hadith, and he said, “This hadith is Hasan sahih.” Ahmad related it as well (3654), but in truncated form.”

"1 Related by Bukhaaree (5974), Muslim (2743), Ahmad (5937), and Abu Daawood (3387).

and Sunnah. The Quran is Allah’s speech ~ the highest and most noble speech. And the Sunnah too is revelation, which Allah 3 inspired the Prophet # with. Not only in supplications, but in general, the Prophet # was blessed with epigrammatic speech ~ speech that consisted of few words but many, profound meanings. Therefore, we do not doubt that a person who invokes Allah 8 with supplications taken from the Qur’ai; and Sunnah is more likely to have his supplications answered than a person who uses other supplications. Both the Qur’an and Sunnah contain many supplications, and though we cannot present all of them in this book since they are so many, we will now mention a few of them. You should notice that each of the following examples comprehends many good meanings.
content_id:
Ketika seseorang berdoa dengan pelan dan diam-diam, ia lebih mungkin untuk merasakan konsentrasi penuh, dengan hatinya benar-benar terpusat dalam pikiran penuh du'a dan kerendahan hati. Sebaliknya, ketika seseorang mengeraskan suaranya, hatinya cenderung menjadi terganggu.

Ketika seseorang berbicara dengan pelan, ia menunjukkan bahwa ia merasakan kedekatan dengan orang yang ia ajak berkomunikasi, berbeda dengan seseorang yang memanggil dengan suara keras kepada orang yang berada di kejauhan. Inilah mengapa Allah memuji hamba-Nya Zakariyyah, dengan firman-Nya:

"Ketika ia memanggil Tuhannya (Allah) — sebuah panggilan dalam diam" (Qur'an 19:3)

Seseorang yang berdoa dengan pelan dan diam-diam kemungkinan akan berdoa lebih lama dibandingkan seseorang yang berdoa dengan suara keras; yang terakhir mungkin merasa bosan berbicara keras-keras, mungkin merasakan kekeringan di mulutnya dan berhenti, atau lidahnya mungkin cepat lelah, sehingga ia akan berhenti.

Terkadang, perlu untuk menyembunyikan nikmat-nikmat tertentu, karena bagi setiap nikmat ada orang yang dengki, yang rasa dengkinya sebanding dengan besarnya nikmat tersebut. Dan salah satu nikmat terbesar adalah diberi taufik untuk berdoa kepada Allah; oleh karena itu lebih baik untuk berdu'a kepada Allah secara diam-diam.

Bab - 22 Adab-Adab Du'a 479

9) Berdoa Dengan Hati Yang Sadar Dan Penuh Perhatian Menyebabkan Du'a Seseorang Dikabulkan

Ketika seseorang berdu'a kepada Allah dengan hati yang terjaga dan waspada,

ia lebih mungkin untuk dikabulkan du'anya. Ini berarti

bahwa seseorang harus membaca doa dengan, antara lain,

rasa takut, harap, dan ketergantungan kepada Allah. Allah berfirman:

"Serulah Tuhanmu dengan rendah hati dan suara yang lembut." Dan Allah berfirman:

"Dan berdoalah kepada-Nya dengan rasa takut dan harap" (Quran 7:56) Dan Nabi bersabda,

be tS Cots Vat OF bg BENG Off 815 a 1250 GY pe i

"Ketika kamu berdu'a kepada Allah, hendaklah kamu meyakini dengan pasti bahwa du'amu akan dikabulkan. Dan ketahuilah bahwa Allah tidak mengabulkan du'a dari hati yang lalai dan lengah."1

10) Dianjurkan Untuk Berdu'a Kepada Allah Dengan Tekun Dan Berulang-Ulang

Sekali lagi, seseorang harus mengambil langkah-langkah yang diperlukan untuk meningkatkan kemungkinan dikabulkannya du'anya. Salah satu langkah tersebut adalah berdu'a kepada Allah dengan ketekunan, berulang kali mengucapkan du'a yang sama, menunjukkan kerendahan hati dan ketergantungan penuh kepada Tuhannya. Dikatakan bahwa siapa pun yang mengetuk pintu cukup sering, cepat atau lambat akan diizinkan masuk.

'Umar Ibn Al-Khattab berkata, "Ketika hari Badr tiba, Rasulullah memandang orang-orang kafir, yang

1 Diriwayatkan oleh At-Tirmidzi (3479), dan Al-Albani menyebutkannya dalam As-Silsilatus-Shahihah (594).

berjumlah 1000 orang, sementara para sahabatnya berjumlah 319 orang. Nabi Allah kemudian menghadap kiblat, mengangkat kedua tangannya, dan mulai berdoa kepada Tuhannya: 'Ya Allah, penuhilah untukku apa yang telah Engkau janjikan kepadaku. Ya Allah, wujudkanlah apa yang telah Engkau janjikan kepadaku. Ya Allah, jika Engkau menghancurkan kelompok ini dari kalangan umat Islam, Engkau tidak akan disembah di muka bumi.' Beliau terus berdu'a kepada Tuhannya, menghadap kiblat, dengan kedua tangan terangkat, hingga jubahnya jatuh dari bahunya. Kemudian Abu Bakr mendatanginya, mengambil jubahnya, dan mengembalikannya ke bahunya. Abu Bakr kemudian memeluknya dari belakang dan berkata, 'Wahai Nabi Allah, cukuplah du'a dan seruanmu kepada Tuhanmu, karena Dia akan menyempurnakan untukmu apa yang telah Dia janjikan kepadamu.' Kemudian Allah 'Azza Wa-Jall (Maha Mulia dan Maha Agung Dia!) menurunkan wahyu: {(Ingatlah) ketika kamu memohon pertolongan kepada Tuhanmu dan Dia mengabulkannya (seraya berfirman): "Sesungguhnya Aku akan menolong kamu dengan seribu malaikat yang datang berturut-turut."}. Maka Allah menolongnya dengan para malaikat..."

Abu Hurairah meriwayatkan bahwa At-Tufail Ibn 'Amr Ad-Dausi datang kepada Rasulullah dan berkata, "Sesungguhnya suku Daus telah mendurhakai dan menolak (menerima Islam), maka mohonkanlah kepada Allah agar mengutuk mereka." Rasulullah kemudian menghadap kiblat dan mengangkat kedua tangannya; sementara itu orang-orang berkata, "Kaum itu (yang dimaksud adalah suku Daus) pasti binasa." Namun Rasulullah memanjatkan du'a ini:

tag, 1555 38 a pg lbs te38 81 Gan "Ya Allah, berilah hidayah kepada [kaum] Daus [suku] dan bawalah mereka.

Ya Allah, berilah hidayah kepada Daus dan bawalah mereka (kepada Rasulullah dan kepada Islam)."2

1 Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim (1763), Ahmad (208), dan At-Tirmidzi (3081). 2 Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhari (2937), Muslim (2524), dan Ahmad (7273). Dan redaksi yang disebutkan di atas berasal dari riwayat Ahmad.

Bab - 22 Adab-Adab Du'a 481

11) Memohon Dengan Tekad Dan Kesungguhan

Ketika seseorang berdu'a kepada Allah, ia harus menunjukkan tekad dan kesungguhan, yang berarti bahwa ia tidak boleh membuat terkabulnya du'anya bergantung pada kehendak Allah, dengan berkata, "Ya Allah, berikanlah aku ini dan itu jika Engkau menghendaki." Sebaliknya seseorang harus menunjukkan tekad, dengan yakin bahwa Allah akan mengabulkan du'anya. Dengan bersungguh-sungguh dan yakin dalam du'anya, seseorang menunjukkan bahwa ia meletakkan tawakkalnya kepada Allah, bahwa ia sedang berdu'a kepada Maha Melihat dan Maha Mendengar, bahwa Tuhannya berkuasa atas segala sesuatu, dan bahwa tidak ada sesuatu pun di langit dan bumi yang dapat melemahkan, menggagalkan, atau mengalahkan Allah.

Anas meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah bersabda,

gb Sieh Be Vy ced 3 ited ai yes Bp "Ketika kamu berdu'a kepada Allah, maka bersungguh-sungguhlah dalam

du'amu. Dan janganlah salah seorang dari kalian berkata, 'Jika Engkau menghendaki, maka berikanlah aku,' karena sesungguhnya tidak ada yang dapat memaksa Allah."

Muslim meriwayatkan hadith ini dengan redaksi: "Tetapi hendaklah ia bersungguh-sungguh dalam meminta dan hendaklah ia meminta sesuatu yang besar (seperti surga), karena tidak ada sesuatu pun yang Allah berikan yang terlalu banyak atau terlalu besar bagi-Nya."

"Maka bersungguh-sungguhlah dalam du'amu" dapat berarti, "Bertekadlah dan janganlah ragu"; sebagian ulama mengatakan bahwa artinya adalah, "Bertekadlah dan janganlah lemah dalam meminta"; dan sebagian lain mengatakan bahwa artinya adalah, "Berprasangka baiklah kepada Allah, dengan yakin bahwa Dia akan mengabulkan du'amu." Dengan berkata, "Ya Allah, berikanlah aku ini dan itu jika Engkau menghendaki," seseorang seolah mengatakan bahwa ia sebenarnya tidak membutuhkan bantuan Allah maupun sesuatu yang ia minta.

1 Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhari (7464), Muslim (2678, dan riwayat kedua:

12) Dianjurkan Untuk Mengawali Du'a Dengan Pujian Kepada Allah Dan Kemudian Shalawat Kepada Rasul-Nya

Para ulama sepakat bahwa dianjurkan untuk terlebih dahulu memuji

dan mengagungkan Allah serta bershalawat kepada Rasulullah

sebelum dan sesudah berdu'a. Fudhalah Ibn 'Abid berkata,

"Rasulullah mendengar seorang laki-laki berdoa dalam shalatnya,

tanpa memuji Allah dan tanpa bershalawat kepada

Nabi. Rasulullah kemudian berkata [kepadanya],

"Kamu terburu-buru [dalam du'amu], wahai hamba. Ketika kamu shalat dan kemudian duduk, pujilah Allah dengan pujian yang layak bagi-Nya dan bershalawatlah kepadaku; kemudian berdoalah kepada-Nya."

Kemudian seorang laki-laki lain shalat setelah itu; ia memuji Allah dan bershalawat kepada Nabi. Nabi pun bersabda,

"Wahai hamba, berdoalah, niscaya kamu akan dikabulkan."1

1 Diriwayatkan oleh At-Tirmidzi (3476), yang berkata, "Hadith ini Hasan"; oleh

Bab - 22 Adab-Adab Du'a 483

Ibn Mas'ud meriwayatkan kisah serupa, di mana ia berkata, "Suatu ketika aku sedang shalat sementara Abu Bakr dan 'Umar bersama Nabi. Ketika aku duduk, aku memulai dengan memuji Allah kemudian bershalawat kepada Nabi. Kemudian aku berdu'a untuk diriku sendiri. Nabi kemudian bersabda,

"Mintalah, niscaya kamu akan diberi; mintalah, niscaya kamu akan diberi."1

13) Memohon Agar Du'anya Dikabulkan Dengan Menyebutkan Amal Shaleh Yang Pernah Dilakukan

Seseorang meningkatkan peluang dikabulkannya du'anya ketika ia menyebutkan amal shaleh yang pernah dilakukan sebelum berdu'a. Nabi meriwayatkan kisah tiga orang laki-laki, yang terjebak dalam sebuah gua ketika sebuah batu besar menutup pintu masuknya. Mereka berkata satu sama lain, "Pikirkanlah amal shaleh yang kalian lakukan karena Allah, kemudian berdu'alah kepada Allah dengannya; semoga Allah kemudian membukakan jalan keluar bagi kita." Ahmad meriwayatkan kisah ini dengan redaksi: "Maka hendaklah setiap orang berdu'a dengan amal terbaiknya; semoga Allah menyelamatkan kita dari [kesulitan] ini."1 Kemudian salah seorang dari mereka menyebutkan amal terbaik yang pernah dilakukannya, setelah itu ia berdu'a kepada Allah; dua orang lainnya melakukan hal yang sama, kemudian Allah mengabulkan du'a mereka dan menyelamatkan mereka dari kehancuran.

14) Dianjurkan Untuk Berdu'a Kepada Allah Dengan Du'a Yang Maknanya Mencakup Semua Kebaikan

Du'a yang paling komprehensif dalam makna kebaikannya adalah du'a yang terdapat dalam Al-Qur'an

Abu Daawood (1481); oleh An-Nassaaee (1284), dan lafaz yang disebutkan di atas berasal dari riwayatnya tentang hadith tersebut. Dalam menilai hadith ini, Al-Albaanee berkata, "sahih." (1217)

1 Diriwayatkan oleh At-Tirmidhee (593); lafaz yang disebutkan di atas berasal dari riwayatnya tentang hadith tersebut, dan ia berkata, "Hadith ini Hasan sahih." Ahmad juga meriwayatkannya (3654), namun dalam bentuk yang diringkas."

"1 Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (5974), Muslim (2743), Ahmad (5937), dan Abu Daawood (3387).

dan Sunnah. Al-Quran adalah firman Allah ~ ucapan yang tertinggi dan paling mulia. Dan Sunnah pun merupakan wahyu, yang Allah ilhamkan kepada Nabi #. Bukan hanya dalam du'a, tetapi secara umum, Nabi # dianugerahi kemampuan berbicara dengan ungkapan yang ringkas ~ ucapan yang terdiri dari sedikit kata namun mengandung makna yang banyak dan mendalam. Oleh karena itu, kita tidak meragukan bahwa seseorang yang berdoa kepada Allah 8 dengan du'a yang diambil dari Al-Quran dan Sunnah lebih mungkin dikabulkan du'anya dibandingkan seseorang yang menggunakan du'a lain. Baik Al-Quran maupun Sunnah mengandung banyak du'a, dan meskipun kita tidak dapat menyajikan semuanya dalam buku ini karena jumlahnya yang sangat banyak, kita akan menyebutkan beberapa di antaranya. Perlu diperhatikan bahwa setiap contoh berikut mengandung banyak makna yang baik.
content_ar: عندما one supplicates quietly and in secret, he is more likely to feel concentrated, with his القلب fully preoccupied in thoughts of الدعاء and التواضع. من ناحية أخرى, عندما one raises his voice, his القلب is likely to become distracted. عندما a person speaks quietly, he shows that he feels a sense of closeness to the one he is communicating with, as opposed to a person calling out in a loud voice to someone who is at a distance, This is why الله 3 praised His slave Zakariyyah %8, with His saying: “عندما he called out his Lord (الله) - a call in secret’ (Qur’an 19:3) A person who supplicates quietly and in secret is likely to supplicate for a longer period of time than a person who supplicates out loud; the latter might get bored of talking out loud, he might feel dryness in his mouth and stop, or his tongue might get tired quickly, and لذا he will stop. Sometimes, من الضروري to keep certain البركات hidden, لأن for every البركة there is a jealous person, whose الغيرة corresponds in degree to the greatness of the البركة. And one of the greatest of البركات is being guided to supplicate to الله 38; ومن ثَم it is better to invoke الله 3% in secret. الباب - 22 The الآداب Of الدعاء 479 9) To Supplicate With An Alert And Attentive القلب Leads To A Person’s Supplications Being Answered عندما one invokes الله 3@ with a القلب that is awake and alert, he is more likely to have his supplications answered. This means that ينبغي للمرء read invocations with, among other things, a sense of الخوف, الأمل, and ete الله 3€ said: “Invoke your Lord with sont and in secret.” And الله %¢ said: as OB ABS) “And invoke Him with الخوف and الأمل’’ (القرآن الكريم 7:56) And النبي ﷺ 2% said, be tS Cots Vat OF bg BENG Off 815 a 1250 GY pe i “عندما you invoke الله, you should believe with certainty that your الدعاء will be answered. And know that الله does not answer a الدعاء from a القلب that is inattentive and heedless,""1 10) يستحب To Invoke الله 3€ Persistently And Repeatedly Again, ينبغي للمرء take the necessary steps to improve the likelihood of his supplications being answered. One such step is to invoke الله % with persistence, repeatedly making the same الدعاء over and over again, showing one’s التواضع and complete dependence upon his Lord. قيل that whoever knocks a door often enough will sooner or later be let inside. ‘عمر Ibn Al-Khattaab 4 said, “عندما the day of Badr came to pass, الرسول ﷺ of الله #% looked at the disbelievers, who "I Related by At-Tirmidhee (3479), and Al-Albaanee mentioned it in AsSilsilatus-Saheehah (594). were 1000 in number, بينما his الصحابة were 319 men. النبي ﷺ of الله #¢ then faced the القبلة, extended his hands, and then began to call out to his Lord: ‘O الله, carry out for me that which You promised me. O الله, bring to pass that which You promised me. O الله, إذا You destroy this group from the people of الإسلام, You will not be worshipped on earth.’ He continued to call out to his Lord, facing the القبلة, and with his hands extended, حتى his robe fell down from his shoulders. Then أبو بكر # went to him, took his robe, and flung it back onto his shoulders. أبو بكر 4 then hugged him from behind and said, ‘O النبي of الله, sufficient for you is your supplicating and calling out to your Lord, for He will complete for you that which He promised you. Then الله ‘Azza Wa-Jall (Mighty and Majestic is He!) revealed: {(Remember) عندما you sought help of your Lord and رد you (saying): “I will help you with a thousand of the الملائكة each behind the other (following بعضهم بعضاً) in succession.”}. لذا الله helped him with the الملائكة..." أبو هريرة 4s related that At-Tufail Ibn ‘Amr Ad-Dausee went to الرسول ﷺ of الله #% and said, “Verily, the Daus (tribe) has disobeyed and refused (to accept الإسلام), لذا invoke الله against them.” الرسول ﷺ of الله #% then faced the القبلة and raised his hands; meanwhile the people were saying, “The people (referring to the Daus tribe) are surely destroyed.” But الرسول ﷺ of الله 3 made this الدعاء: tag, 1555 38 a pg lbs te38 81 Gan “O الله, guide the [people of the] Daus [tribe] and bring them. O الله, guide Daus and bring them (to الرسول ﷺ of الله 3% and to الإسلام).""7) 'l Related by مسلم (1763), أحمد (208), and At-Tirmidhee (3081). 1 Related by Bukhaaree (2937), مسلم (2524), and أحمد (7273). And the above-mentioned wording is from أحمد’s narration of the الحديث. الباب - 22 The الآداب Of الدعاء 481 11) To Ask With Determination And Resolve عندما one invokes الله 4, he should show a sense of determination and resolve, أي that he should not make the answer of his الدعاء hinge upon الله’s will, by saying, ‘“O الله, grant me such and such إذا you will.” Instead ينبغي للمرء show resolve, being sure that الله 3¢ will answer his الدعاء. By being determined and sure in one’s supplications, one shows that he places his trust with الله 3%, that he is invoking the All-Seeing and the All-Hearing, that His Lord is upon all things able, and that nothing in the heavens and earth can weaken, thwart, or defeat الله 3¢. أنس related that الرسول ﷺ of الله # said, gb Sieh Be Vy ced 3 ited ai yes Bp “عندما you invoke الله, then be determined in your الدعاء. And do not let one of you say, ‘إذا You will, then give me,’ for indeed, none can coerce الله.” مسلم related the الحديث with this wording: “But let him be determined in asking and let him ask for something great (مثل الجنة), for nothing that الله 3% gives is much or great for Him.’ “Then be determined in your الدعاء” can mean, “Be resolved and do not hesitate”; some العلماء say that it means, “Be resolved and do not be weak in asking”; and yet others say that it means, “Think well of الله, being sure that He will answer your supplications.” By saying, ““O الله, give me such and such إذا you will,” one seems to say that he doesn’t really need either الله to help him or the thing he is asking for. "] Related by Bukhaaree (7464), مسلم (2678, and the ثانياً narration: 12) It ls Recommended To Preface One’s Supplications With Praises For الله 3 And Then The Sending Of الصلوات Upon His Messenger 2% العلماء unanimously agree that يستحب to أولاً praise and extol الله 3@ and send الصلوات upon الرسول ﷺ of الله 3 قبل and بعد one supplicates. Fudaalah Ibn ‘Abeed 4 said, “الرسول ﷺ of الله # heard a man supplicate in his الصلاة, without glorifying الله and without sending الصلوات upon النبي ﷺ #%. الرسول ﷺ of الله #% then said [to him], “You have rushed [your الدعاء], O worshipper. عندما you pray and then sit, praise الله with praises He is deserving of and send الصلوات upon me; then invoke Him.’ Then another man prayed بعد that; he praised الله and sent الصلوات upon النبي ﷺ #%. النبي ﷺ # said, “O worshipper, supplicate, you will be answered.’”"] {] Related by At-Tirmidhee (3476), who said, “This الحديث is حسن’; by الباب - 22 The الآداب Of الدعاء 483 Ibn Mas’ood & related a similar narration, in which he 4 said, “I was حين praying بينما أبو بكر and ‘عمر # were with النبي ﷺ #. عندما I sat down, I began by praising الله and then sending الصلوات upon النبي ﷺ #%. I then supplicated for myself. النبي ﷺ # then said, “Ask, you will be given; ask, you will be given.””") 13) Asking For One's Supplications To Be Answered By Dint Of Previously Performed الخير Deeds One increases the chances of his الدعاء being answered عندما one mentions a previously performed الخير deed prior to supplicating. النبي ﷺ #% related the story of three men, who were stuck in a cave عندما a large rock blocked off its entrance. قالوا to بعضهم بعضاً, “Think over the الصالح deeds you performed for الله, and then invoke الله by them; perhaps الله will [then] make an opening for us [to leave].”” أحمد related the narration with this wording: “Then let each man invoke by the best deed he performed; perhaps الله will save us from this [predicament].”"] Next, one of them mentioned the best deed he performed, بعد which he invoked الله 3€; the other two followed suit, and then الله 3 answered their supplications and saved them from destruction. 14) يستحب To Invoke الله 3 With Supplications Whose Meanings Are Comprehensive Of All That Is الخير Supplications that are most comprehensive in their meanings of goodness are those supplications that are found in the Qur’an Abu Daawood (1481); by An-Nassaaee (1284), and the above-mentioned wording is from his narration of the الحديث. In grading the الحديث, A\- Albaanee said, ““صحيح.’ (1217) 1 Related by At-Tirmidhee (593); the above-mentioned wording is from his narration of the الحديث, and قال, “This الحديث is حسن صحيح.” أحمد related it as well (3654), but in truncated form.” "1 Related by Bukhaaree (5974), مسلم (2743), أحمد (5937), and Abu Daawood (3387). and السنة. The القرآن الكريم is الله’s speech ~ the highest and most noble speech. And the السنة أيضاً is الوحي, which الله 3 inspired النبي ﷺ # with. Not only in supplications, but in general, النبي ﷺ # was blessed with epigrammatic speech ~ speech that consisted of few words but many, profound meanings. لذلك, we do not doubt that a person who invokes الله 8 with supplications taken from the Qur’ai; and السنة is more likely to have his supplications answered than a person who uses other supplications. Both the Qur’an and السنة contain many supplications, and though we cannot present all of them in this الكتاب بما أن they are لذا many, we will now mention a few of them. You should notice that each of the following examples comprehends many الخير meanings.
